text: During a left-sided laparoscopic nephrectomy, the surgeon attempts to apply a Hem-o-lok clip to the dissected pedicle. However, the clip applicator malfunctions after loading the clip, preventing its application.
The surgeon ties the pedicle using a silk thread, passing it behind the vein and pulling it out from the upper end to secure the knot, compensating for the malfunctioning hemoloclip applicator.
The surgeon ties the first surgical knot around the pedicle, ensuring the knot's direction is corrected for secure ligation.
The surgeon places multiple knots using silk thread on the pedicle, followed by the application of three hundred titanium clips to secure the vessel.

The surgeon places one clip above and one below the knot on the renal vein, then clips the upper part of the vein towards the kidney to prevent retrograde blood flow. The renal vein is then cut, revealing one knot and two clips securing the vessel.
